description:
# Compiler2 CLI: add fz2 binary front door

Goal: publish a real outer CLI surface for Compiler2 so run/build/interp are no longer stranded behind unit tests.

Change:
- add a new `fz2` binary with the same backend-facing verbs: `run`, `build`, `interp`, `help`
- route those verbs straight into Compiler2-owned front doors instead of old planner/module entry points
- keep the old `fz` binary untouched for now
- share only the truly generic AOT link step rather than duplicating it
- add end-to-end binary tests for `fz2 run`, `fz2 interp`, and `fz2 build`

Design constraints:
- no `run2` / `build2` / `interp2` command sprawl inside `fz`
- no quiet fallback from `fz2` back into old planner/codegen discovery
- diagnostics may stay coarse, but must come from Compiler2 telemetry rather than fabricated old-world state

Signal:
- `cargo test --test fz2_cli -- --nocapture` passes
- `fz2` binary paths do not emit `[fz, planner]` or `[fz, type_infer]` telemetry when exercised through Compiler2-native fixtures
title: Compiler2 CLI: add fz2 binary front door
